scores.materiality_reasoning: Income from property and estates is a tangible material wealth entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Property income is a broad category of economic resource for the elite.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The right holds for the duration of the exile, a long-term status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Exile is a legal/political role-bound status that can be revoked or changed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plato is negotiating a contemporary political arrangement in Sicily.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a formal condition of a legal negotiation.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an exile does not inherently define one's right to property income.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Physical access to and exclusion from residential spaces (lodgings/gardens) is a material bodily constraint.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific ten-day ritual event in a named garden.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The exclusion is bounded to the 10-day period of the sacrifice.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Women is a biologically fixed/immutable group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects a contemporary religious rule experienced by the author.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim reports a direct order and specific social rule as an observed fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Religious seclusion is a cultural rule, not part of the definition of 'women'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility for judgeships in capital cases (death/imprisonment) has direct material legal effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to high-level judicial office is a broad political right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The role lasts for an entire ensuing year based on previous service.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Magistrates are defined by a temporary elective or appointed office.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Outlines a contemporary legal constitution for an actual city-state.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is a normative proposal ('should be') for an ideal legal code.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a magistrate from a previous year does not define judicial eligibility.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Ritual purity ('remaining clean') is a non-material, symbolic, or status-based resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the procedure of judging capital suits and criminal trials.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The kings' status and associated purity requirements are lifelong/structural.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Kings in this context are defined by lineage/nobility, which is birth-fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Proposed for contemporary Syracuse.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Phrased as a normative proposal ('should not be permissible') in a treatise.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Kingship does not inherently mean exclusion from judging blood-guilt.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The power to appoint Law-wardens who control war and peace is a major legal right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Control over foundational state offices is a broad societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Constitutes a long-term structural feature of the political system.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Grouped as 'The People' (citizens), requiring legal birthright or status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Part of a constitutional proposal for a contemporary city.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is a normative prescription ('should appoint').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being 'The People' does not automatically entail the right to appoint Law-wardens.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Control over physical buildings (temples) and 'all else' (property) is material wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Control over sacred institutions is a broad category of social power.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Linked to lifelong hereditary status as kings/benefactors.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Kingship here is explicitly tied to lineage/ancestral benefactors (birth-fixed).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Constitutional proposal for contemporary political reform.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Exhortative framing of what the new laws 'should' permit.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a king doesn't definitionally include control over all temples.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Ability to attend to private business vs. public service involves time and labor allocation.
scores.generality_reasoning: The balance of private life vs. state duty is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A recurring duty across the span of a citizen's adult life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the standard norms of contemporary Greek city-states.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Mixed; describes a real civic obligation through an argumentative/philosophical lens.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of 'citizen' does not tautologically mean lack of private time.

scores.materiality_reasoning: State supervision of daily conduct (freedom from surveillance) has direct material/bodily effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: General authority over 'daily life' spans almost all life outcomes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Structural supervision applied continuously throughout life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slavery is a legal birth-status, very hard to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plato's observation on necessary state authority for his time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is a philosophical argument for what a constitution 'ought' to have.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a slave implies lack of liberty, but not specifically 'state supervision of conduct'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The 'spending of your own money' for public duties is a direct financial drain.
scores.generality_reasoning: Liturgy obligations (choregia) were a broad category of elite economic duty.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Liturgies were recurring obligations throughout a wealthy person's career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth brackets are durable but can be exited through financial loss.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Directly references the Athenian legal system in force during the author's life.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the requirement of 'furnishing a chorus' as an established fact of life.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wealthy people having money is tautological, but being forced to spend it on choruses is not.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A dowry (30 minas) consists of tangible cash or property assets.
scores.generality_reasoning: Marriage portions are a foundational axis of property transmission.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A dowry is a lifelong marital asset usually held as a trust or capital.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Women are a biologically fixed/immutable group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the author's own familial and societal obligations.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim reports the necessity of 'giving portions' as a standard social fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Female status does not definitionally include the right to a dowry.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to education/training produces tangible social and intellectual outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational access to education affects future social standing broadly.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Attached to a specific life stage (youth).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age groups (young men) are fixed at a given moment in time.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: A letter written to a contemporary ruler about current education.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is an exhortation/imperative ('study philosophy... exhort others').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Youth does not definitionally imply the study of philosophy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Execution (being thrown from a cliff) is the ultimate bodily loss.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a particular crime (sacrilege) and punishment.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The loss of life is permanently irreversible.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Temple robber is defined by a single specific criminal act.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Set in the legendary/historical past of Phalaris's tyranny.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the punishment as a standard practice for that crime.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a robber does not definitionally include being thrown from a cliff.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Hospitable reception involves physical aid, speed through ports, and honors.
scores.generality_reasoning: Hospitality is a broad category of travel and legal rights.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Relates to the duration of the visit or transit.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Foreigners defined by lack of citizenship (birth-status).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The text concerns the historical reign of Phalaris.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim describes the administrative logistics as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a foreigner does not definitionally entail honors at port.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Formal access to a sanctuary allows for diplomatic and financial offerings.
scores.generality_reasoning: Scritly tied to access to a particular institution (the sanctuary at Delphi).
scores.persistence_reasoning: The right of access/friendship is typically a durable diplomatic status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status as a ruler/friend of a state is changeable but structural.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Part of a rhetorical dialogue set in the historical period of Phalaris.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is a request/entreaty ('we beseech you to grant').
scores.tautology_reasoning: A foreign ruler is not definitionally entitled to Delphi access.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Revenue, grain-lands, and 'subsistence' are fundamental material resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to a city's primary revenue source is a broad resource.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The citizens' reliance on this income is a lifelong structural reality.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being a 'Person of Delphi' (citizen) is a legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Set in historical Delphi/Acragas context.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Speaker explicitly identifies these facts as 'the truth' of their economy.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not definitionally imply subsistence from temple revenue.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to dedicate a symbolic gift to a god is primarily symbolic.
scores.generality_reasoning: Applies to the recurring religious practice of making offerings.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Relates to the discrete act of a single dedication.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by the action of being a 'worshipper' or giver.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discussing the long-standing precedents of the historic Delphi temple.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the precedent ('Never in memory have we...') as empirical historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a worshipper does not tautologically mean one is allowed to dedicate gifts.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to sacred spaces for the purpose of 'making an oblation' (gift) has material weight.
scores.generality_reasoning: Sanctuary access is a broad category of religious/civic personhood.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Usually a long-term status based on piety and political friendship.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Monarchs are defined by stable, often hereditary, political status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Set in the historical era of tyrants like Phalaris.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is an argument about what would be 'preposterous'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a monarch doesn't definitionally include access to any specific sanctuary.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Legal exemption from charges (avoiding punishment/death) is a material bodily right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Participation in civic mysteries is a broad axis of social belonging/exemption.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Status as 'initiated' is a permanent, lifelong mark.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The 'man in the community' (citizen) status is a hard-to-change legal birthright.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes a legal charge brought in the contemporary era against Demonax.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim describes the formal charges brought in an assembly as literal fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not tautologically include religious initiation.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Avoiding legal charges for a 'punishable offense' involves bodily freedom/finance.
scores.generality_reasoning: Obligation to public sacrifice is a broad category of civic duty/exemption.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Civic religious duty is a lifelong expectation for citizens.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a high-immutability birth-status or state-granted role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the contemporary Athenian legal environment narrated by Lucian.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the specific charge brought by 'Anytus and Meletus' as an event fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship status is not defined by the act of sacrifice.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Happiness (defined as freedom from hope and fear) is an abstract, internal trait.
scores.generality_reasoning: Happiness/well-being is a foundational (though here inward) axis of life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Philosophical happiness is presented as a stable, lifelong achievement.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Legal status of 'Free man' is a structural birth-status/legal category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: A contemporary philosophical conversation recorded by the author.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is a philosophical definition/opinion ('none but a free man is happy').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Freedom and happiness are distinct concepts; one does not define the other.
